---
title: "Methodology"
description: "How The SABRhood defines, validates, and communicates its baseball metrics."
---

::: {.page-intro}
<span class="eyebrow">Open methods</span>

# Trust the number because you can inspect it

Every published product has a defined grain, information cutoff, sample threshold, and method label.
:::

{{< include includes/methodology-data.html >}}

## Core rules

1. Raw play-by-play remains outside the public website.
2. The site receives compact derived products with one documented row grain.
3. Pregame analysis never uses information from later in the game.
4. Estimated and proxy statistics are labeled as such.
5. Small samples receive reliability fields instead of false certainty.
6. Automated story candidates remain subject to editorial review.

## Current analytical layers

- Canonical pitch, plate-appearance, batted-ball, and pitcher-appearance views.
- Empirical 24-state run expectancy and linear run value.
- Hitter and pitcher results, contact quality, and platoon splits.
- Pitch shape, discipline, and within-plate-appearance sequencing.
- Recent performance compared with a non-overlapping earlier baseline.
- Bullpen workload availability and exploratory pitcher-hook modeling.
